revision.text:
[[ja:息石耳命]][[th:เจ้าชายโอกิมิ]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Amatoyotsuhimenomikoto|qid=Q10940731|1=Amonotoyototsu-hime}}|father={{ill|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}|mother={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|qid=Q11561868|1=Nunasokonakatsu-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|1=Emperor Itoku}}}} {{Nofootnotes|date=October 2010}}
'''Genealogical chart based on the ''Nihon Shoki''''' {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|02| 01={{Supra|3}}{{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}|02=[[Nunasoko Nakatsuhime|{{color|#FC4E6B|Nunasoko Nakatsuhime}}]]}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|^|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||!| 01='''Okisomimi no Mikoto'''}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01=[[Ame Toyotsuhime no Mikoto|{{color|#FC4E6B|Ame Toyotsuhime no Mikoto}}]]|02={{Supra|4}}{{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!|}} {{familytree|border=0||||01| 01={{Supra|5}}{{ill|Emperor Kōshō|en|Emperor Kōshō|qid=Q312841|lt=Emperor Kōshō|1=Emperor Kōshō}}}} {{familytree/end}}
'''Okisomimi no Mikoto''' (息石耳命/おきそみみのみこと or いきそみみのみこと, dates of birth and death unknown) was an ancient {{ill|Japanese|en|Japanese|qid=Q346080|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese}} [[:en:Imperial family|imperial prince]] mentioned in texts such as the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. He was the first prince of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}, the third emperor, and his consort [[Nunasoko Nakatsuhime]]. His siblings included {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Emperor Itoku|1=Emperor Itoku}} (the fourth emperor), and his daughter [[Ame Toyotsuhime no Mikoto]] became Empress of Emperor Itoku. According to the ''[[:en:Sendai Kuji Hongi|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'', Okisomimi no Mikoto had no heirs. One version of the ''Nihon Shoki'' refers to Emperor Annei’s first prince as '''Tokotsuhikoirone''' (常津彦某兄), which aligns more closely with the names of his mother and daughter. The ''[[Kojiki]]'' identifies him as '''Tokonetsuhikoironemikoto''' (常根津日子伊呂泥命). ==Genealogy== ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== '''Ikisomimi no mikoto''' is a relative of early Japanese Emperors. See the family tree below for more info. ==Genealogy== {{Generations of Jimmu}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== ===References=== {{translated page|ja|息石耳命|version=99563453|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Ikisomimi no mikoto]] [[th:เจ้าชายโอะกิมิ]] [[ja:息石耳命]] {{wikidata link|Q11492323}} [[Category:Imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
